scope.summary: Implement keyframeable Tack Strength (tack_strength and tack_compliance tracks) in MaterialAnimationTracks and expose tailor_keyframe_tack_strength MCP tool to match MD 2025.2 animatable tack constraints
acceptance_criteria: MaterialFrameParams in tailor-solver/src/params.rs MUST include tack_strength: f32 (0.0 = detached, 1.0 = full strength) alongside tack_compliance: f32, matching spec TR-7.3 exactly

MaterialAnimationTracks MUST promote tack_compliance from post-MVP stub to a fully implemented HashMap<String, KeyframeTrack<f32>> keyed by tack_id, plus a global tack_strength: KeyframeTrack<f32> for uniform override

The animation sim-over-timeline loop MUST compute tack_strength per frame from the keyframe track and perform a partial GPU buffer write (GpuTackConstraint.strength_scale fields only, not a full buffer re-upload) per spec TR-7.3

A tailor_keyframe_tack_strength MCP tool MUST be registered accepting garment_id, tack_id, frame, strength [0,1]; it MUST write to tailor_animations.animation_json via CRDT update and emit TailorAnimationDraftUpdated

Setting tack_strength=0.0 at a keyframe MUST cause the trim to detach from the cloth at that frame; TRIM_ATTACHED ValidationDescriptor MUST be advisory (not blocking) for frames where strength=0.0 is explicit in the track
TailorSimRunCompleted payload MUST include the full tack track data in its MaterialFrameParams array for reproducibility

A trim-animation integration test MUST assert that animating tack_strength from 1.0 to 0.0 at frame 15 causes the trim to be unattached at frame 16 and correctly re-attached when strength returns to 1.0 at frame 25
scope.risk_if_missed: MD 2025.2 Tack constraint animation is the primary mechanism for stitch-and-unstitch effects (e.g., a jacket collar lifting off, a button-down shirt coming undone over time). The spec (TR-7.3, spec line ~5107) explicitly requires tailor_keyframe_tack_strength as a named MCP tool and tack_strength in MaterialFrameParams. MT-019 provides static tack only; MT-287 wires MaterialKeyframe but the struct does not include tack_strength; MaterialAnimationTracks stubs tack_compliance as post-MVP. This leaves a named spec requirement unimplemented and breaks the Trim group animation contract.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
